Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.

Level 1: you have used 55 actions; a strong score needs about 32 or fewer. You are 1.7x over the target.

Also, maybe I should try clicking on the white area between the checkerboard and the buttons, or on the border of the checkerboard.

Or what if I need to click on the white border/frame of the checkerboard?
Let me try clicking on the white border around the checkerboard.

# Current button area

print("\nCurrent button area:")
for r in range(42, 49):
    print(f"Row {r}: {curr_lines[r]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 12, 'col': 30}])
print("Result:", last_action_result)
print("Board changed:", last_action_result.get('board_changed'))

if last_action_result.get('board_changed'):
    prev_lines = previous_frame.ascii.split('\n')
    curr_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
    changed_rows = [r for r in range(len(prev_lines)) if r < len(curr_lines) and prev_lines[r] != curr_lines[r]]
    print(f"Changed rows: {changed_rows}")
    for r in changed_rows:
        if r != 1:  # skip progress bar
            print(f"Row {r} prev: {prev_lines[r]}")
            print(f"Row {r} curr: {curr_lines[r]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
